descript.abstract:
This data set is the result of an effort to identify hospitals both licensed to operate in Minnesota or significant to Minnesota citizens. For example, major hospitals in Fargo, North Dakota and La Crosse, Wisconsin are included due to their proximity to population centers in Minnesota that are closer to those facilities than other hospitals in Minnesota.
The main source of licensed hospitals is the Directory of Licensed and Certified Health Care Providers at the Minnesota Department of Health: https://gisdata.mn.gov/dataset/health-facility-providers
Additional non-Minnesota locations were manually sourced from open directories and public map databases.
descript.purpose: To provide state agencies and the general public with a basic overview of the locations of major hospitals that serve the general population, and provide links back to the Minnesota Department of Health licensing systems for pertinent details.

timeperd.current: Data was downloaded and geocoded in March 2020.

idinfo.useconst: The use of this data is conditioned on acceptance of the MDH terms and conditions in the Distribution Liability section.

overview.eaover: OBJECTID - sequential identifier HFID - Identifier for MN Department of Health Directory of Licensed and Certified Health Care Providers (positive numbers). Otherwise assigned sequentially (negative numbers) NAME - Name of the hospital - as licensed in Minnesota, as locally known outside Minnesota ADDRESS - Address of the facility CITY - City of the facility STATE - US State of the facility ZIP - Postal code of the facility TELEPHONE - key contact phone number FAX - Fax number

distinfo.distliab:
Data is provided "as-is". The Minnesota Department of Health makes no warranty, expressed or implied, and disclaims all implied warranties of the data for any purpose. MDH also does not warrant that the data is error free. The data was developed for MDH internal business purposes. MDH shall not be liable for any activity involving the data with respect to the following: a.) Lost profits, lost savings or any other consequential damages; b.) The fitness of the data for a particular purpose; or c.) The installation of the data, its use or the results obtained.
MnGeo's data disclaimer: https://www.mngeo.state.mn.us/chouse/disclaimer.html

stdorder.ordering: This data set is distributed via the Minnesota Geospatial Commons and is accessible via the online linkage below.

procstep.procdesc:
Processing steps completed by Mike Dolbow, Minnesota Department of Education:
1. Downloaded from Minnesota Department of Health database - chose Hospitals, all counties, all types: https://gisdata.mn.gov/dataset/health-facility-providers 2. Removed header, deleted non-standard county columns, deleted empty columns 3. Geocoded with Esri World Geocoding Service 4. Manually reviewed against imagery, Google Maps, and Open Street Map imports 5. Moved point to building when warranted by manual review 6. Deactivated columns best sourced from MDH licensing information 7. Added several records "just across the border" using various sources. Assigned negative HFID numbers and filled in available information.
